rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in implementation scope when binding to implementation-specific behavior, after subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by anchoring contracts to the scope of the target component or implementation, not its subcomponents.
Ensure annex content references only features or properties visible at the target scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use boolean helper declarations for range checks (e.g., x >= min and x <= max) to ensure type consistency.
Avoid direct subcomponent port references in contracts unless mediated by the parent's interface.
anti_patterns: Do not inject annex blocks into component types when the constraint depends on implementation-specific bindings.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
